title: Software install request failed
description: Software install request failed Reported problem: I requested new software and the installer will not complete. Context: I can help troubleshoot that with you. Do you see a permissions error, package error, or a general install failure?

messages.text: I requested new software and the installer will not complete.
I can help troubleshoot that with you.
Do you see a permissions error, package error, or a general install failure?
Hi, This is an approved software request, but the installer says I do not have permission.
I checked the related account settings as well.
I reviewed the software deployment policy and the application was not targeted correctly to your machine.
After assigning the package properly to your device, the installation finished without errors.
Hi, I can access it now.
Happy to help. I am resolving the ticket now.

ground_truth.resolution_summary: The software install failed because the package deployment scope did not include the device. Correcting the deployment restored installation. The user expressed urgency during the conversation.

title: Cannot join secure wireless
description: Cannot join secure wireless Reported problem: Hello, I cannot join the secure office wireless network anymore. The guest Wi-Fi works, but the secure one does not. thanks Context: Hello, I can help troubleshoot that with you. Do you see a password prompt, certificate warning, or just repeated disconnects?

messages.text: Hello, I cannot join the secure office wireless network anymore. The guest Wi-Fi works, but the secure one does not. thanks
Hello, I can help troubleshoot that with you.
Do you see a password prompt, certificate warning, or just repeated disconnects?
it is the secure staff wi-fi and it drops every few minutes.
Hello, I reviewed the recent configuration changes tied to this service.
Hi, I reviewed the endpoint configuration and the machine certificate used for secure Wi-Fi is no longer valid.
After renewing the device certificate and reapplying the Wi-Fi profile, the connection stabilized.
Looks good now. I can use it again.
Happy to help. I am resolving the ticket now.

ground_truth.resolution_summary: The laptop was using an expired machine certificate for the secure SSID. Updating the certificate and Wi-Fi profile resolved the issue.

title: Recover previous file version

It works now, thank you.

ground_truth.resolution_summary: A previous file version was available in backup retention. Recovering that copy restored the needed data. The user expressed urgency during the conversation.

I reviewed the device storage and most of the space is tied up in temporary local profile data.
I cleared the oversized local cache and temp files, and the device now has usable free space again.
Looks good now. I can use it again.

ground_truth.resolution_summary: Low disk space was caused by accumulated temporary or profile cache data. Cleaning that data restored usable free space.

title: Service-to-service API error
description: Service-to-service API error Reported problem: One internal service is failing when it calls another API. Context: Hi, Hi, I can help with this. Did the issue begin after a recent deployment to either service?

messages.text: One internal service is failing when it calls another API.
Hi, Hi, I can help with this.

The logs suggest the API response format changed and the client no longer handles it. The workflow breaks on one API call consistently.

The workflow still fails and the error is unchanged.
I reviewed the traces and the upstream API contract changed in a way the client service is not handling.
There may actually be a second issue happening at the same time. I cannot browse normal websites from my work laptop.
What exact error do you see in the browser when a page fails?
Internal sites are okay, but external websites fail with a proxy error.
I rolled the client configuration forward to the current API contract and the integration started working again.
Looks good now. I can use it again.
Glad to hear it. I am marking this resolved. let me know what you see

ground_truth.resolution_summary: Service-to-service calls were failing because the upstream API response changed. Aligning the downstream parser with the new contract resolved the issue. The user expressed urgency during the conversation. A secondary issue related to internet_access was also reported.
